title: Guitar Pocket Reference: CAGED Chords & Pentatonic Scales
topic: Guitar practice reference: CAGED chords and pentatonic scales — a small, focused, keep-it-open-while-you-play book.

concepts.title: How to read these diagrams (chord boxes, ASCII tab, finger numbers, muted strings)

notes:
Structure this as a practice reference book, one two-page spread per chord or scale.

LEFT PAGE = the playable artifact: a chord-box/grid diagram AND monospace ASCII guitar tab, with finger numbers and muted-string markers (x/o). This is the thing the guitarist looks at while playing.

RIGHT PAGE = a short, to-the-point explanation: the shape, when/why it's used, common transitions or licks, and one practice tip. Keep it terse — this is a practice book, not a prose book. No verbose paragraphs; prefer short lines and bullets.

ORDERING: chords in CAGED order (C, A, G, E, D open shapes first, then the 5 movable CAGED barre shapes). Pentatonics ordered by real-world use: Minor Pentatonic Box 1 first, then Boxes 2–5, then Major Pentatonic, then the Blues scale.

WIDGET: a single sticky metronome shared across the whole book (not one instance per page) — it should stay accessible as the reader flips spreads. Adjustable BPM (range ~40–240), start/stop, tap-tempo, audible click with an accent on beat 1, 4/4 default. Use the shared book-widgets.js verified-widget runtime. Anchor it bottom-left near the playable page.

Keep the book small and focused (~18 spreads + a one-page cheatsheet summarizing all shapes/boxes at a glance).

Requested voice: A concise guitar teacher — terse, practical, no filler. Speaks like margin notes on a practice sheet.

Requested look: Clean, print-like light theme, like a spiral-bound practice book on a music stand. Page-turn transitions.
